briefdescription.para: Initializes the gaze tracker, setting up camera and models.

simplesect.para: True if initialized successfully, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Stops camera acquisition and model inference pipelines.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to emit lifecycle status change signals.

briefdescription.para: Clears active calibration, reverting to uncalibrated geometric projection.
Maps logical viewport coordinates to primary screen logical coordinates.
Filters raw gaze coordinates using configured smoothing algorithms.
Manually injects raw gaze origin and direction coordinates.

parameterdescription.para: Gaze direction unit vector in camera-space.

briefdescription.para: Gets the current head rotation in OpenCV Camera Space (Euler angles in degrees).
Gets the current head translation in OpenCV Camera Space (in millimeters).

memberdef.argsstring: (Vector3 origin, Vector3 direction, bool apply_calibration=true) const
memberdef.name: project_gaze_ray_to_viewport

briefdescription.para: Projects a 3D ray in Camera Space (in millimeters) onto the physical screen plane, and maps it to viewport/window-local pixel coordinates by subtracting the window position.

parameterdescription.para: The 3D start point of the ray in Camera Space.
The 3D direction vector of the ray in Camera Space.

simplesect.para: The 2D viewport-local pixel coordinates of the intersection point, or Vector2(INFINITY, INFINITY) (resolving to Vector2.INF in Godot) if the ray is parallel to or points away from the screen.

briefdescription.para: Gets the left eye center position in OpenCV Camera Space (in millimeters).
Gets the right eye center position in OpenCV Camera Space (in millimeters).
Gets the gaze direction unit vector in OpenCV Camera Space.
Godot Node coordinating camera tracking, model inference, and gaze coordinate projection.

detaileddescription.para: GazeTracker handles camera acquisition (via CameraSensor), pipelines inputs through YuNet face detection and GazeNet estimation (via Face/Eye Estimators), smoothes projected points, and maps coordinates from camera space (millimeters) to viewport space (logical screen pixels).
